reviews: Good professor if you're taking data science. Extremely knowledgeable and caring, the labs are not as bad as people make it out to be if you just read the instructions. He is very willing to help during office hours and loves when you're curios.

Dr. Smith is a very caring and knowledgeable professor. When you need help, he is always willing to help during class and in his office hours. He responds to emails pretty quickly and is super understanding when you have difficulties and will extend deadlines.

Professor Smith wasted lecture time yapping about stuff that was not even relevant to the content. He would do labs towards the end of class and none of the content made sense. He barely explained the code. There were group projects, especially the end of the semester. He had too much energy, especially for an 8AM class. Def do not recommend him!

Generally a very good professor, my main problem was his lack of answering emails. I probably sent 15+ all semester and only got a response to one which was not helpful. Other than that though he is very helpful, always will extend due dates if you just talk to him! Hes very nice and so smart!!

Honestly, I did not learn much from Professor Smith and relied on the TA's often, but he is extremely nice and fun to talk too. Very encouraging and doesn't grade hard. I think if you're taking a class other than Data Science he would be a great professor.

Smith cares about the subject he teaches. He provides a lot of resources, including his own handmade primer for the course in addition to homework solutions. Homework isn't graded heavily, and there hasn't been any quizzes 7 weeks in yet. Each exam gets a 6x4in notecard. Cares about student success and subject, just needs to communicate it better

He's a chill guy during lab period. I'll give him a 5 if he'd been more transparent with our grades. I was left with the final letter grade without knowing the scores for most of my reports and no feedback at all, so I don't know if there's any mistake or he just gave the grade randomly. And most importantly, he can never be reached through email.

If he teaches chem tech lab, take him!!! He is a chill guy and he knows how to deal with technical stuff that may occur in the lab. He takes a while to grade reports but trust me, he is not a harsh grader. You'll get at least a B and above if you know how to write a detailed report. Final was chill for me all multiple choices.

Awful! nice man but awful teacher. Attending lecture is pointless when this man just rambles about what he's going to discuss and once he finally does, he does not explain the material and make it easy to grasp.  The curve is not that big. Overall, I would not recommend. Chem 2 is a difficult subject and requires a good teacher !!!

He's a very kind person, yet I wasn't a good student for him. His teaching and lectures were really not meant for me.. maybe it's different for others. I wasn't faring well in the beginning because I believed I could learn sole from him, but then I attended another his lecture and a another lecture, did practice problems, then I gradually improved.

He's a teacher you would need to see if you were confused on one of the chapters. he's a good teacher, but also a tough teacher who's exams are very hard. but he does grade on a curve if everyone did poorly. If you're going to take him, go to class EVERYDAY and take the lecture SERIOUSLY, if you want to pass.

Took him for Chem 1 and Chem 2 and had to retake both. Took Bloxton the second time around and got all A's. Although he is a nice guy, his tests are extremely hard and most of the averages are around 55.  Does Not answer emails. Super hard to reach.

Kids would skip his class to go to other teacher's lectures. Unclear and rambled, but a friendly man who would help if you needed it. Everyone talked about how they regretted taking his class and the class averages for tests hovered around a 65. Curved at the end, but I honestly learned nothing.

This man tries really hard to be a good teacher but it just isnt for him. He should really stick to research or something because he wasted a whole semester of mine. His exams are unbelievably hard in comparison to his horribly poor teaching skills. Bloxton who is an amazing professor gives exams that makes sense. Would never take Smith again.

Smith is a good person, just not a good teacher. You could tell that  he wanted to teach you, but didn't know how. His knowledge was higher than General Chem, and didn't know how to break things down and teach to students. He would present you with the information and if you asked how to do it, he struggled because it came 2nd nature to him.

Many stupid kids take his classes and he gives a HUGE curve. so basically a C on his exams are an A. Other than that, I dont go to lecture cause its pointless and you need to teach yourself. I have a chem backgroud so its easy but idiots mostly struggle in his class so ride the curve if you're good in chem :)
Failed 35% of my Spring '14 class but I did not think it was that bad. Did the book problems and practice test a week before the exam and got a B.
He's not difficult at all as long as you do masteringchem and try his in-class examples and practice tests. Had 70 m/c questions for the final but was still manageable.

really nice guy but does not know how to teach. Going to lecture doesn't really help. Best way to study for this class would be doing textbook chapter problems. The one good thing is his curves are really big so as long as you beat the class average on his exams you should do ok.

Really nice guy but horrible teacher who doesn't know how to teach. Going to lecture doesn't really help much. Best advice to study for his exams is to do book problems. Gives really big curves so as long as you beat the class average you should do ok in his class.

Hard class, but Smith will meet with you anytime, anywhere to try to help you. Grades is based mostly on doing the homework and the 3x5 notecard allowed for formulas on the test. Test questions from homework. Smith is nice but super-slow in his lectures. Be prepared, this is a quantum physics course, even if not stated.

His lectures are a bit boring but his tests are easy. If you just do the problems in the back of the chapter and outline the chapters you'll get an A. A lot of mastering chem problems but same with all the other professors. This was one of my favorite classes.

This teacher is so busy with his research, that he has no time for his students.  If he cannot spend time and effort to prepare his lectures, then my learning will be limited.  Very disappointed with my final grade for the class.

I had this guy for Tech 1 and he was SO boring and only taught from the slides. He's a physical chemist so he's a little more interesting in 3105 but not much.  He's so smart but he can't explain it so you can understand. He also takes forever to grade and post helpful material so I feel like I'm set up to fail. Expect to learn alot on your own.

Not the most interesting material but he does his best to get through it. Go to class, take notes and do book problems thats it! Ppl that complain on here dont go to class and expect an A. Gotta put in some effort and do book problems bc he does cram alot on exams but they are very manageable as long as you practice. Overall its a pretty easy A
2nd time taking Chem 1032, and his exams are a lot easier than Dr. Price's. However, he's an extremely dry lecturer. It's your choice.

I can't stand being in his class. They make you buy the textbook and the solutions Manuel, but then he doesn't assign problems from the book. He just makes up random ones, which are tweeked differently then the book so you are in the dark constantly. Also he does not teach. You'll sit there for 50 minutes and get nothing from it.

Don't know why there's a lot of hate for this guy. Easy class, go and see him during his office hours and he'll help you out. Read the textbook, do problems in the back, and you'll be on your way to an A.

If you are going to take him, be prepared to teach yourself chem. His class isn't that bad at all if you do study and know the material real well. All exams are multiple choice but don't get so happy.

Such a BAD teacher. He does not know how to teach and does not get any teaching done. He takes the first 30 minutes of class going over what you learned the class before. You will never be prepared for his exams because he doesnt go over most of it he just rambles in class on pointless information. I recommend to NOT take him if you plan to learn.

Professor Smith is a terrible teacher. You can literally not come to class and wouldn't miss anything. He wastes too much time telling you what you should do to prepare and rambling instead of teaching you. Does not provide adequate reviews for tests and most times you will be lost when it comes to lab. Does not allow retests either.

Probably the worst professor I've had thus far here at Temple. MasteringChemistry takes hours to complete and the lectures usually consist of a summary of the little material that was gone over the previous class as well as some new material. Seems like a nice guy, but horrible teacher. I wouldn't recommend him.

I feel as if all the bad comments are coming from Gen Chem 1 students and that is true! I'm an honest person to tell you, even though this guy may seem nice that doesnt make up for who you feel after every exam. Masteringchem is your life. Most of his exams are multiple choice but dont be too happy!!

Just because Dr. Smith has a Ph.D. does not mean he is a good teacher. His lectures have nothing to do with the exams (which are extremely difficult and math-based). He also assigns Mastering Chem. problems every week which are also difficult and time consuming, but they are more like the tests. I highly advise against taking him. Spare yourself.

Tech has the potential to be a hard class; Dr. Smith made it an easy A. Exams were insanely easy compared to book problems.  He did his best to cover a boring subject (I love chem, but tech is really not interesting), so I don't know why people complain about his teaching. Very kind and will help if you ask him.

He means well, but truly has no idea how to approach teaching.  He will lecture on a single slide for 3 class periods.  Also, he summarizes one before beginning the powerpoint and then the slide on the powerpoint. Out of a 50 min class he summarized for 25 on avg.  I would say avoid him, but he was easy.

Dr. smith is a nice person, hes there for help if you need him in his office hours. Hes exams are very difficult and have little to do with his lectures. Masteringchemistry is assigned every week and will be your life! But be sure to do them because about 1/3 of the questions appear on the exam which is multiple choice. basically your on you own!

I don't know what the other people below me are saying he is NOT good guy! He tries every possible way to fail you in order to keep his job! Reading the book doesn't help and he can't teach at all! He is nice on the outside but not on the inside. AVOID!

go over the notes, attend every class, do the problem sets and ask questions. Buy the ACS exercise booklet coz u won't get much info about it online like the orgo chem. Smith will help u every time u have questions in his "Office hour". Read thru the book and take key points from it, thats how i got my A-

Terrible professor. He does not tell you straight forward things to focus on for the exam and his questions end up being along the lines of drawing a stupid figure from the book. There is no curve in the class at all. His tests do progressively get easier but if you screw up one test you're ultimately screwed for a decent grade.

He seems like a nice guy. Always came in with a good attitude. Eager to teach. His notes were hard to study from so I stuck with the book. Exams were brutal but there was a big curve. I stayed 10-20 points above the average and finished with a B. Final is ACS, also brutal.
Decent Professor. Hard. I got an A-.

Dr. Smith is a great guy and gives the student a challenge. For all the people out there who talk about about his Gen chem I course, their comments are irrelevant. To get the A, do as follows: read the book, do mastering chemistry, do EVERY problem in the back of the book. Problem solving is ESSENTIAL.

Nothing against Smith personally, I just feel he has chosen the wrong profession. He is an awful teacher and although the class is passable you learn nothing. His average test grades are in the 50's and he blames the students but if everybody is doing that poorly it is clearly a reflection upon the teaching. If you want to learn, don't take him.

Smith is  passionate teacher. His TA is helpful also. He wants students to go extra miles. His grading is generous. In order to do good on his exams, the students need to complete his homework sets. seek for his TA?s help on the hard problems.Try to understand the topics as much as possible instead of memorizing the materials  mechanically.

The thing is for some people Chemistry isnt easy and for others its a piece of cake. This guy really knows his stuff, but you will have to put your butt down and actually study and read over material to get the grade you want, its not high school kids! Go to his office and he will help you, he is very helpful if you go to his office!And do your HW!

Smith is a very smart individual, but too smart for his own good. You essentially teach yourself. The material covered in lecture is nothing like the material included on tests. The tests are nearly impossible to pass. I would recommend taking someone else.

Nice guy. Definitely smart, but lacks teaching skills. At Temple you're supposed to teach yourself chem, so the lecturer is really more of a motivator than a giver of knowledge. Smith is not a good motivator. You could increase your chances of becoming interested in chem 1 by taking Dr. Price. Smith is inconsistently inconsistent in his approach.

This is essentially a self-taught course.  Smith is approachable and friendly, but he only outlines what you need to know, he doesn't teach the content.  His exams are needlessly elaborate.  Expect to spend four or more hours on each homework assignment.  He doesn't teach the fundamentals of chemistry, just problem solving.  Nice man, poor teacher.
He's really really really bad.  This guy is the worst professor temple has to offer...at least from my experience

His tests are nearly impossible!! The averages are usually around a 50%. Reading the book to study for the test doesn't help at all. He gives a lot of mastering chem problems and they are hard. I recommend not taking him if you can help it.

A nice guy, but his lectures are extremely boring and slides are straight from the book.  Going to his lectures are absolutely unnecessary because you really don't learn from them.  Basically, you're on your own to learn the material from your book.  Test are ridiculous, but you can't expect anything less.  And masteringchemistry is IMPOSSIBLE.

Ridiculously hard exams which he does not prepare students for. His lectures are merely the tip of the iceberg as far as material covered on the exams. If you take him, you are on your own to learn the material...good luck!

Dr. Smith is a nice guy but not the best teacher. He covers very little in class, and almost all of my learnig was done by myself. His tests were ridicolous and impossible to do in the time that was alloted for them. The plus is that if you have any questions he'll help you during office hours and it's not too hard to get some points back on tests.
A good teacher and a good person.

Dr. Smith is a very fair professor. Yes, I know his test are hard and lengthly, but he is very clear to what is expected and explains topics well in class. Try your best and good things usally will come your way.

Yeah, the tests are difficult, but chem tests are supposed to be difficult. What really matters is that prof Smith does a good job of explaining the material so that you understand the basis of what's going on, and actually cares that you know the stuff. I've had other chem profs at Temple, and in retrospect he was my favorite.

Did not really explain how to do the problems for the online homework (Mastering Chem). His tests are extremely lengthy and the tests were moderately difficult. MASTERING CHEM IS YOUR LIFE!! KNOW IT WELL!!
Good teacher but tests were a little difficult. I'd take him again.

Dr. Smith is passionate about his subject. Though he's a new to Temple, he clearly is not new to teaching. The lectures are very helpful, and he does not take attendance. The tests are not needlessly difficult (as they are in some other chem classes...). As for the user who complained about a 60 average, um, it's called a bell curve... duh.
He sucks. wastes time in class and puts questions on the test that werent up for review. one test the class avg was a 60. find someone else.
Automatic A, I highly recommend him!

Decent professor, really good about answering questions people ask in class. Most if not all of what he goes over in lecture just outlines what's in the book - skipping lecture is pretty common. The tests are easy (multiple choice). Online homework is more difficult but allows for multiple tries, so you can get over 100% even if you miss questions.
Easy professor. Tests are not too difficult, but his mastering chem hw can be quite challenging. All said, you will not regret taking him at all!!1
Great professor. His lectures are clear and he puts together good power points. Tests and quizzes are fair and you will do fine if you go to lecture.
